title: DIKI Hierarchy Interactive Pyramid
description: An interactive four-level pyramid visualizing the DIKI Hierarchy (Data, Information, Knowledge, Insight) using a single hospital-patient scenario that progresses from a raw temperature reading to an AI-derived readmission insight. Hover or click to reveal a definition and a concrete example at every layer.
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Information Systems
Data Hierarchy
Knowledge Management
learningObjectives: Name the four layers of the DIKI Hierarchy in order from bottom to top
Classify a given example as Data, Information, Knowledge, or Insight
Explain why most failed IS projects fail at a layer transition
Describe how DIKI differs from the older DIKW Hierarchy

controls.description: Hover over pyramid layers to see definitions and examples
Click on layers to pin the callout open

assumptions: Four-level DIKI variant of the DIKW hierarchy
'Insight' is preferred over 'Wisdom' because it names a deliverable rather than a virtue
limitations: Single hospital-patient scenario; does not show alternative domains
Does not depict feedback loops back from Insight to Data
